{"id":"smallhold-3ie","title":"Inbound Delete: remove posts/actors, handle unknown gracefully","description":"Handle Delete{Note}: remove from remote_posts (and associated mentions, notifications referencing that post). Handle Delete{Actor}: remove remote_accounts entry and all their posts, follows, and follower relationships. Activities targeting unknown objects: return 200 silently — never 404.

**Agent workflow**: Spawn a research subagent to capture Delete activity fixtures from mastodon.social and document exact structure.

**Test discipline**:
- Delete known post → removed from remote_posts
- Delete unknown post → 200, no error
- Delete actor → remote_accounts row removed, their posts removed, their follows removed
- Verify Delete is signed by the author (not by a different actor)
All fixtures captured from real servers. Real in-memory SQLite for DB tests — never mock the database.

**Defensive programming**:
- Verify Delete.actor matches the actor that owns the deleted object
- Use ON DELETE CASCADE or explicit transaction for multi-table cleanup
- Rate-limit Delete processing — a burst of Delete activities can indicate a misconfigured server or an attack
- No .unwrap() outside test code; all errors are Result<T, AppError>","acceptance_criteria":"Delete known post removes it from remote_posts; Delete unknown post returns 200; Delete actor cascades to their posts and follows; signature ownership verified; rate limiting applied","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:29:09Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:29:09Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-v0z","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:29:12Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
